config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because that can easily be used to justify abuse of power and silence people who are vulnerable. In my view, a good society protects shared interests while still treating individual rights as a real boundary, not something to sacrifice whenever it is convenient. From my perspective, the best balance is collective responsibility with strong personal freedoms.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu, karena dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika Serikat, itu terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Pengalaman politik dan hukum di sini menunjukkan bahwa hak individu justru sering menjadi pagar utama ketika mayoritas atau pemerintah ingin melangkah terlalu jauh. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya demi efisiensi atau kenyamanan sosial. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menekan individu, melainkan yang melindungi individu sambil tetap mengejar kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement, because in Indonesia I have seen how “society first” can be used to pressure people into silence, conformity, and obedience. Collective interests matter, especially in a country as diverse and interdependent as ours, but they should not erase basic rights like free expression, due process, and protection from abuse. If individual rights are treated as secondary, the people with the least power are usually the ones who suffer first. For me, a healthy society is one that serves the common good without making the individual a disposable sacrifice.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu, karena dari pengalaman Amerika Serikat, “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan. Benar bahwa masyarakat butuh aturan dan tanggung jawab bersama, tetapi hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum yang adil, dan perlindungan dari kekuasaan mayoritas tidak boleh dianggap kalah penting hanya karena dianggap mengganggu ketertiban. Kalau hak individu bisa disingkirkan kapan saja atas nama kepentingan sosial, yang paling rentan biasanya justru orang biasa, bukan elite. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat adalah masyarakat yang mampu mengejar kepentingan bersama tanpa menjadikan individu sebagai korban yang bisa dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still disagree with the statement, because from an Indonesian perspective “society first” can too easily become a tool for forcing conformity and protecting authority. We do need social harmony and mutual responsibility, but in practice the people who get hurt first when rights are downgraded are usually the weak, the unpopular, or those who speak up. In a diverse country like Indonesia, individual rights are not a selfish luxury; they are the safeguard that keeps the common good from turning into pressure and abuse. For me, society should serve people, not ask individuals to disappear for the sake of a vague collective interest.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu, karena dari perspektif saya di Amerika Serikat, kepentingan masyarakat terlalu mudah dipakai sebagai alasan untuk menekan kebebasan individu dan memperluas kekuasaan negara. Saya paham argumen tentang ketertiban, harmoni, dan kebaikan bersama, tetapi pengalaman politik di sini menunjukkan bahwa mayoritas tidak selalu benar, dan hak individu justru berfungsi sebagai batas yang mencegah penyalahgunaan. Kalau hak bisa dikorbankan kapan saja demi “kepentingan sosial,” yang paling sering jadi korban adalah orang biasa, bukan mereka yang punya kuasa. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menempatkan individu di bawah kolektif, melainkan yang mengejar kebaikan bersama tanpa merampas hak dasar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
